title: Token-telemetry: anchor transcript attribution to the assembler marker ("Artifact path: <path>") instead of a bare substring — prevents cross-phase over-attribution when a phase's prompt cites another phase's deliverable
summary: Follow-up to the ArtifactPath-primary attribution fix (fix/token-telemetry-artifactpath-attribution, scanner.go attributes()). attributes() now matches strings.Contains(firstUserText, ArtifactPath). go-reviewer found a real (currently-narrow) over-attribution vector: some profile prompts cite OTHER phases' artifacts in their own first user message (e.g. .evolve/profiles/retrospective.json:118 'Read .evolve/runs/cycle-{cycle}/build-report.md and audit-report.md'). A bare-substring match would attribute such a transcript to the builder's Window if that phase is ever routed onto a claude* driver (any-CLI-any-phase invariant) and its untimestamped lines slip past withinWindow. Fix: match the literal marker the assemblers stamp — subagent.go:350 'Artifact path: %s' / run.go:384 '- Artifact path: %s' — i.e. match ('Artifact path: ' + ArtifactPath), which excludes prose mentions while matching every real launch. NOTE: this DELIBERATELY updates the protected S1 contract test TestTranscriptScan_ConcurrentSessionsSameDir_OnlyContentVerifiedCounted (scanner_test.go), whose fixture uses a bare artifact-path-in-message; update it to the marker form as part of the change and document why. Currently low blast radius (retrospective defaults to agy-tmux, excluded from the transcript tier; a phase's usage is scanned right after its OWN launch before later transcripts exist) but it silently undermines this campaign's accuracy goal if it ever fires.
acceptance: attributes() matches the assembler marker, not a bare path substring
a fixture where phase B's first user message merely REFERENCES phase A's ArtifactPath does NOT attribute B to A's Window
every real production launch (INVOCATION CONTEXT header) still attributes
S1 contract test updated to the marker form with a rationale comment
